message:
feat(messaging): semantic execution lane planner for SubjectCell conversation families

Adds a semantic execution lane planner above the canonical SubjectCell
ownership boundary for reasoning about conversation-family parallelism.

src/messaging/fabric.rs — Introduces ReorderingLaw and ParallelIssueLaw
enums with fail-closed defaults. Adds ProtocolKernel (protocol-level
concurrency contract with interference classes, obligation footprint,
reordering/parallel-issue laws), SemanticConversationFamily (one
conversation family routed within a subject cell with conflict detection
via interference class, obligation footprint, and shared-state footprint
overlap), SemanticExecutionLane (deterministic lane grouping families
that must serialize), and SemanticLanePlan (with serial_work_units() and
projected_parallel_rounds() for quantifying parallelism benefit).

SubjectCell::plan_semantic_execution_lanes() partitions families into
lanes using connected-component traversal over the conflict graph, with
deterministic ordering by scheduling pressure. Two families conflict
when their interference classes overlap, obligation footprints overlap,
or shared-state footprints overlap.

Includes 3 tests: explicit-parallel-contract-to-split verifies that
non-conflicting families land in separate lanes, hot-namespace
decomposition verifies conflict detection via shared-state footprint,
and projected parallel round reduction verifies deterministic
re-planning and parallelism quantification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
